acceptance_surface: Power BI Desktop PBIX canvas; HTML preview was not used as the acceptance surface
final_polish_scope: KPI row grid realigned to the chart-card content grid
KPI SVG card made full-bleed inside the image visual with no separate visual shell

layout_decisions.reason_for_296_width: The true content grid spans x=24 to x=1256, width=1232. Four 300px KPI cards leave only 10.7px gaps and cause the right card to feel out-of-grid. Four 296px KPI cards plus three 16px gaps fill the grid exactly.

current_lens_polish.desktop_result: chips are visibly larger, centered, and do not crop or overlap the Current Lens context text

kpi_svg_polish.renderer: native Power BI image visual bound to SVG Image URL measures
kpi_svg_polish.visual_shell: disabled for KPI images so the SVG itself is the only visible card border/background
kpi_svg_polish.sparkline: true SVG polyline with filled area, dashed reference line, start marker, and end marker
kpi_svg_polish.desktop_result: KPI cards fill the visual container more tightly and no longer look like a smaller card sitting inside a larger outer box

qa_rounds.notes: Full-page review shows KPI row aligned inside the report grid. No card is out of the right edge; card 1 aligns with chart left edge and card 4 ends at chart right edge.
Revenue var and Close risk chips are visibly larger, centered, and readable without clipping.

qa_rounds.surface: Power BI Desktop canvas screenshot after Escape/unselect
qa_rounds.notes: KPI SVG cards fill the image containers more tightly; no scrollbar, white header, measure name, or nested visual-shell border is visible.
Changing BU to Contract Logistics changed Revenue from about $9.7M to $2.5M, updated charts and Current Lens context, and rendered Close Risk zero-state as $0.0M without breaking the SVG card.
KPI cards and Current Lens retained the same aligned grid and no crop/scrollbar/header leakage.
KPI cards and Current Lens retained the same aligned grid. Decision/Control Signals did not return and lower visuals remain clear.

validation.asset_build: python tools/build_native_pbix_assets.py regenerated model/model.bim and build/native_report_layout_project13.json
validation.pbi_tools_extract: pbi-tools extract output/dashboard_final.pbix completed successfully after final repack

residual_notes: Power BI Desktop can still show selection handles while a visual is selected; this was excluded from layout judgment by pressing Escape/unselecting before QA.
The final KPI width is 296 rather than 300 because 296 is the largest clean width that preserves a 16px equal gap and exact right-edge alignment inside the chart grid.
